name: product-ui-system
summary: A mature interface-led marketing archetype built around modular cards, reusable interactive primitives, and disciplined hierarchy rather than decorative spectacle.
goals: Make a broad product or platform feel coherent through reusable UI shells.
Show multiple workflows or product areas without losing clarity.
Support several conversion paths while keeping one primary action obvious.
layout_rules: Use a stable shell with a few repeatable module types rather than many bespoke sections.
Alternate between promise, proof, and interaction modules to maintain pace.
Let mobile reprioritize content and interaction patterns instead of only stacking desktop layouts.
typography_rules: Use concise headlines and tightly controlled support copy.
Rely on hierarchy, weight, and spacing rather than expressive display typography everywhere.
Keep metadata and supporting labels visually quiet but still legible.
component_rules: Use modular cards, tabs, calculators, or segmented shells as reusable product-story blocks.
Keep one dominant CTA per page state and make secondary actions clearly subordinate.
Invest in highly reusable surfaces such as search shells, product cards, or scenario selectors.
motion_rules: Use motion to change state, switch panes, expand search, or browse galleries.
Keep autoplay and carousel behavior explicit and controllable.
Reduce motion intensity when clarity and task speed matter more than atmosphere.
copy_rules: Translate abstract platform value into workflows, savings, or immediate next steps.
Use simple action labels such as Get free, Request a demo, Search, Show more, or Subscribe.
accessibility_rules: Use semantic tabs, buttons, inputs, and lists for reusable modules.
Preserve focus order and skip links even in highly customized UI shells.
Make overlays, carousels, and sheets explicitly dismissible and keyboard-safe.
performance_rules: Code-split heavier modules such as media, cards, and calculators.
Prefer a small number of reusable shells over many unique interaction systems.
Defer video and secondary media until they are actually needed.
anti_patterns: Adding too many interactive modules without enough proof or hierarchy.
Letting responsive variants drift so far that the narrative becomes inconsistent.
Using carousels or modular grids when simple stacked sections would be clearer.
Treating marketplace or platform density as an excuse for clutter.
notes: This archetype works well for SaaS platforms, productivity suites, and polished marketplace-adjacent experiences.
Its strength comes from system discipline more than mood or art direction.
